facts.fact: The insurer is Alliance Group Benefits.
The certificate number is AGB-GT-2024-TechCorp-00842.
The master policy number is AGB-2024-TC-GROUP.
The issue date is June 1, 2024.
The certificate holder (employee) name is Alex Morgan Taylor.
Alex Morgan Taylor's employee ID is TC-8842.
Alex Morgan Taylor's department is Software Engineering.
Alex Morgan Taylor's date of birth is February 14, 1990.
Alex Morgan Taylor's date of hire is March 1, 2022.
The employer (policyholder) is TechCorp Industries, Inc.
TechCorp Industries, Inc.'s corporate headquarters is in San Jose, California.
The basic life insurance amount is 2x Annual Salary = $180,000.
The voluntary additional coverage amount is $100,000 (employee-elected).
The total coverage is $280,000.
The effective date of coverage is June 1, 2024.
The employer pays the basic coverage premium.
The employee pays $12 per month for voluntary coverage.
The primary beneficiary is Jordan Casey Taylor (Spouse) at 100%.
The contingent beneficiary is Taylor Family Revocable Trust at 100%.
This certificate is evidence of coverage under the Master Group Policy issued to the employer.
The Master Policy controls in case of any conflict with the certificate.
Coverage is subject to the terms, conditions, and exclusions in the Master Policy and the certificate.
The employee became eligible for coverage on the date of hire.
Basic coverage became effective automatically without requiring evidence of insurability.
Voluntary coverage required enrollment within 31 days of hire.
Late Entrant provisions apply if enrolling after 31 days of hire.
The basic life benefit equals 2 times the annual base salary, rounded to the next higher $1,000.
Voluntary supplemental coverage may be elected in increments of $10,000, up to a maximum of 5 times salary.
The basic benefit will be recalculated annually on the policy anniversary based on current salary.
Total coverage cannot exceed $1,000,000.
Coverage terminates on the date the Master Policy terminates.
Coverage terminates on the last day of the month in which the employee ceases to be an eligible employee.
Coverage terminates on the date the employee ceases active full-time employment.
Coverage terminates on the date the employee requests termination of voluntary coverage.
Coverage terminates on the last day for which required premiums have been paid.
If group coverage terminates for any reason except non-payment of premium, the employee may convert to an individual whole life policy without evidence of insurability.
The employee must apply for conversion within 31 days of termination.
The conversion policy will be issued at the employee's attained age at conversion.
The conversion policy will use standard premium rates for the individual policy.
The maximum conversion amount is the lesser of group coverage or $200,000.
No medical exam or health questions are required for conversion.
If the employee leaves employment, coverage may be ported (continued as an individual policy).
Ported coverage continues at the same amount as under the group policy.
The employee must apply for portability within 31 days of termination of employment.
Portable coverage premium rates will be age-banded group rates, payable directly to the insurance company.
Evidence of insurability is NOT required for portability.
If diagnosed with a terminal illness with life expectancy of 12 months or less, the employee may request acceleration of up to 75% of the death benefit.
The minimum accelerated death benefit amount is $5,000.
The maximum accelerated death benefit amount is $250,000.
The accelerated amount will be reduced by an actuarial discount.
Any amount accelerated reduces the death benefit payable to beneficiaries.
If death occurs by suicide within 12 months of the coverage effective date, no death benefit will be paid.
Group coverage has no pre-existing condition exclusions.
The employee may change the beneficiary at any time by submitting a change form to the employer's HR department.
If no beneficiary survives the insured, benefits will be paid to the estate.
If multiple beneficiaries are named, benefits are divided equally unless percentages are specified.
Beneficiaries should notify Alliance Group Benefits immediately upon death of the insured employee.
Required claim documents include a completed claim form (available from employer or insurer).
Required claim documents include a certified death certificate.
Required claim documents include proof of beneficiary identity.
Claims must be submitted to: Alliance Group Benefits - Claims Department, 1200 Insurance Plaza, Dallas, TX 75201.
The Claims Hotline is 1-888-555-CLAIM.
Benefits will be paid within 10 business days of receipt of complete claim documentation.
If the employee becomes totally disabled before age 60 and remains disabled for 6 consecutive months, voluntary life insurance premiums will be waived.
Coverage continues without premium payment during total disability.
The employee must provide proof of continuing disability annually for the waiver to continue.
The premium waiver ends when the employee returns to work, recovers, or reaches age 65.
This certificate is issued under and governed by the laws of the state of California.
Alliance Group Benefits is located in Dallas, Texas.
The authorized signatory is the Vice President, Group Operations of Alliance Group Benefits.
The certificate was issued on June 1, 2024.
Jordan Casey Taylor's relationship to the certificate holder is Spouse.
